title: ‘I am a Raider’: Maxx Crosby breaks silence after Ravens trade falls through
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Maxx Crosby's trade to the Ravens fell through due to Baltimore's concerns about his medical status.
Crosby confirmed his stay with the Raiders in a social media post, expressing his commitment to the team.
The Patriots expressed interest in Crosby as they look to bolster their pass-rushing capabilities.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her grandmother, support cancer patients
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Laura Davis, 35, from Wakefield, Mass., will run the Boston Marathon for Dana-Farber in memory of her grandmother.
Gamma, Laura's grandmother, battled Stage 4 pancreatic cancer; her memory inspires Davis's charity-driven marathon runs.
Davis has completed five marathons since 2019, supporting cancer patients and families; Boston will be her third World Major.

title: Carjacking suspect shot and killed in Roxbury, police say
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston police officers shot and killed a carjacking suspect in Roxbury on Wednesday night, according to officials.
The suspect tried to flee in a stolen vehicle and struck a police cruiser before officers opened fire.
The investigation is ongoing with Boston Police and the Suffolk District Attorney's office leading the efforts.

title: Colin Dorgan, who lost 3 family members in Pawtucket shooting, scores game-winning goal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Colin Dorgan scored the game-winning goal for Blackstone Valley Co-op in double overtime against Portsmouth.
Dorgan's family members were killed in a Pawtucket shooting in February by his mother's ex-husband, Robert Dorgan.
The Blackstone Valley team wears patches commemorating Dorgan's family and aims to support him after the tragedy.

title: ‘We are in the midst of winter versus spring here’: Ski resorts are closing operations, reducing lift prices, limiting lifts
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Ski resorts in Massachusetts and Vermont closed Thursday due to heavy rain and winds.
Wildcat Mountain in New Hampshire closed its Wildcat Express lift and reduced ticket prices on Thursday.
Vermont's Mount Snow and Jay Peak adjusted lift operations due to adverse weather conditions.

title: Two officers rescue woman found asleep amid house fire in Merrimac
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Two officers, Paul DeCoste and Adam White, rescued a woman from a house fire in Merrimac on Wednesday morning.
The resident who reported the fire, Jeff Strogney, saw flames up to 15 feet high and called 911.
Officers entered the smoke-filled house on Bear Hill Road and safely evacuated the only occupant, a woman asleep in bed.

title: A 22-year-old man died in a three-car crash in Canton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A 22-year-old Marshfield man, Mathew McTiernan, died in a three-car crash on I-95 in Canton.
The crash occurred at 6:20 a.m., impacting two of the three lanes of traffic on I-95.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating the cause of the crash involving a Toyota sedan.

title: Baby boomers feeling crushed by housing crunch
author: Lindsay Crudele
bullets: Baby boomers made up 53% of home sellers and 42% of buyers in 2025, according to the National Association of Realtors.
David Bourassa faces a 70% rent increase in Southbridge's Lebanon Hill Housing complex, once income-restricted senior housing.
Massachusetts residents aged 65+ have doubled since 2010, now 23% of the population, as housing needs grow more complex.

title: You can now pass through TSA PreCheck at Logan using only your face. Should that make us nervous?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Boston Logan Airport now offers TSA PreCheck flyers a 'Touchless ID' option using facial recognition to verify identities.
Privacy advocates express concerns over TSA's biometric data use, fearing normalization of mass surveillance without sufficient regulations.
Travelers can opt out of facial recognition; TSA promises not to store or use photos for law enforcement post-security check.

title: Kraft Group, Foxborough announce World Cup funding deal for Gillette Stadium security
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Foxborough and Kraft Sports & Entertainment Group reached a deal on World Cup security funding for Gillette Stadium.
Boston Soccer 2026 will provide advance funding to cover security costs, relieving Foxborough of financial responsibility.
Seven World Cup games are set to take place at Gillette Stadium, beginning June 13 with Haiti vs. Scotland.
